name: Johnny Pascuzzi
headline: Camp body in a crowded Chargers tight end room
body:
Pascuzzi joins Los Angeles on a reserve track, set to fight for a back-end spot in the tight end group once OTAs open. There is no draft capital, no ADP, and no projection attached to his name, and he sits rostered in essentially zero leagues. That is the honest read on a player who signed to compete rather than to start, and nothing about his arrival signals a path to meaningful snaps in a room that already has its pecking order established.

The one mild tailwind is scheduling: his 2026 slate grades out as the eighth-easiest for the position, opening against Arizona, the sixth-softest matchup. But strength of schedule only matters if you are on the field, and Pascuzzi has to win a job first. Treat him as a deep-roster name to monitor through training camp, not a draftable asset. If injuries thin the depth chart ahead of him, revisit; otherwise leave him on waivers.
